variables.name: prompt:customizationsIndex
variables.value:
<skills>
Here is a list of skills that contain domain specific knowledge on a variety of topics.
Each skill comes with a description of the topic and a file path that contains the detailed instructions.
When a user asks you to perform a task that falls within the domain of a skill, use the #tool:read/readFile tool to acquire the full instructions from the file URI.
<skill>
<name>gsd-add-backlog</name>
<description>Add an idea to the backlog parking lot (999.x numbering)</description>
<file>/Users/enwxst0660/.copilot/skills/gsd-add-backlog/SKILL.md</file>
</skill>
<skill>
<name>gsd-add-phase</name>

</skill>

<skill>
<name>agent-customization</name>
<description>**WORKFLOW SKILL** — Create, update, review, fix, or debug VS Code agent customization files (.instructions.md, .prompt.md, .agent.md, SKILL.md, copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md). USE FOR: saving coding preferences; troubleshooting why instructions/skills/agents are ignored or not invoked; configuring applyTo patterns; defining tool restrictions; creating custom agent modes or specialized workflows; packaging domain knowledge; fixing YAML frontmatter syntax. DO NOT USE FOR: general coding questions (use default agent); runtime debugging or error diagnosis; MCP server configuration (use MCP docs directly); VS Code extension development. INVOKES: file system tools (read/write customization files), ask-questions tool (interview user for requirements), subagents for codebase exploration. FOR SINGLE OPERATIONS: For quick YAML frontmatter fixes or creating a single file from a known pattern, edit the file directly — no skill needed.</description>
<file>/Users/enwxst0660/.vscode-insiders/extensions/github.copilot-chat-0.42.2026033001/assets/prompts/skills/agent-customization/SKILL.md</file>
</skill>

<agents>
Here is a list of agents that can be used when running a subagent.
Each agent has optionally a description with the agent's purpose and expertise. When asked to run a subagent, choose the most appropriate agent from this list.
Use the #tool:agent/runSubagent tool with the agent name to run the subagent.
<agent>
<name>gsd-advisor-researcher</name>
<description>Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-assumptions-analyzer</name>
<description>Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-codebase-mapper</name>
<description>Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-debugger</name>
<description>Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-executor</name>
<description>Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-integration-checker</name>
<description>Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-nyquist-auditor</name>
<description>Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-phase-researcher</name>
<description>Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-plan-checker</name>
<description>Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-planner</name>
<description>Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-project-researcher</name>
<description>Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project or /gsd:new-milestone orchestrators.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-research-synthesizer</name>
<description>Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-roadmapper</name>
<description>Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-auditor</name>
<description>Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd:ui-review orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-checker</name>
<description>Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd:ui-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-researcher</name>
<description>Produces UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases. Reads upstream artifacts, detects design system state, asks only unanswered questions. Spawned by /gsd:ui-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-user-profiler</name>
<description>Analyzes extracted session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions to produce a scored developer profile with confidence levels and evidence. Spawned by profile orchestration workflows.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-verifier</name>
<description>Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-advisor-researcher</name>
<description>Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>

<description>Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode.</description>
</agent>

</agent>

<description>Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>Explore</name>
<description>Fast read-only codebase exploration and Q&A subagent. Prefer over manually chaining multiple search and file-reading operations to avoid cluttering the main conversation. Safe to call in parallel. Specify thoroughness: quick, medium, or thorough.</description>
<argumentHint>Describe WHAT you're looking for and desired thoroughness (quick/medium/thorough)</argumentHint>
</agent>
</agents>

invocationMessage.value: Asking 5 questions (Bootstrap, Goal, Features, Research, Constraints)

questions.title: Bootstrap
questions.message: No PROJECT/ROADMAP/STATE files exist yet. Should I bootstrap this as Milestone v1.0 "joint consent change" now?

questions.title: Goal
questions.message: In one sentence, what is the primary user outcome for this milestone? (Example: both joint-account holders must approve critical changes before they take effect.)

questions.title: Features
questions.message: List the top features you want in scope for this milestone (comma-separated).

questions.title: Constraints
questions.message: Any constraints/decisions to lock now? (regulatory, timeline, platforms, backend limits, etc.)

data.92104b2f-6faf-447d-88ed-722f4d51e3c1: joint consent api calls need to be changed. The api for generating qr for joint consent as well as the ai to fetch status is changed, ask for more details and curl of apis

response.value: I captured your milestone intent and will now gather the API-level specifics you requested so requirements and roadmap are precise around the changed QR-generation and status-fetch endpoints.

invocationMessage.value: Asking 5 questions (QR API, Status API, Auth, Behavior, Compat)

questions.title: QR API
questions.message: Share the new QR-generation API details: method, path, request body, response schema, and current curl example.

questions.title: Status API
questions.message: Share the new status-fetch API details: method, path, query/body fields, response schema, and current curl example.

questions.title: Auth
questions.message: What auth headers/tokens and signing rules are required for these APIs?

questions.title: Behavior
questions.message: What behavior changes are required in app flow after these API changes (polling cadence, status mapping, retries, error handling)?

questions.title: Compat
questions.message: Should old API support remain (feature flag/fallback), or do we cut over fully?

data.92f89049-f7bc-4914-a82f-8f2689159d1f: keep in mind the base url will be same accross app following is the curl, curl --location 'http://localhost:8083/smartnow-identity-service/consent-journey-link' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --header 'Accept: application/json' \ --data '{     "mb": {         "operationid": "123444",         "rq": {             "appinfo": {                 "appid": "SmartNow",                 "appver": "1.0",                 "channel": "Mobile"             },             "deviceinfo": {                 "deviceip": "192.168.43.115",                 "devicelat": "",                 "devicelong": "",                 "devicemac": "02:00:00:00:00:00",                 "devicemodel": "samsung-SM-J700F",                 "deviceos": "android",                 "deviceos_version": "6.0.1"             },             "reqdata": {                 "journeyRefNo": "968282106758",                 "mobileNo": "9876543210"             }         }     } }'
data.77e04da5-717c-4f17-9b6a-9ffdc16f14c9: the polling cadence will be as is, retries and error handling will also be the same, status mapping will change as per api response
data.3fe129a7-1880-4647-8cba-fd53cad81f3f: Remove old API support, but only in joint consent, it is being used elsewhere that should remain as is. Also make sure that mb rs wrapping in request is done at interceptor level, only put reqData in the request while calling api

edits.text: SmartNow is a multi-package Flutter platform used to power onboarding and related financial journeys across mobile and web. It combines shared data/network foundations, reusable UI building blocks, and journey-specific frontends in a single workspace. This planning context tracks what the team is building next and why.
Critical onboarding journeys remain reliable and compliant while evolving APIs and backend contracts without user-facing disruption.
## Current Milestone: v1.0 joint consent change
**Goal:** Update joint consent flow integrations to new backend contracts for QR journey-link creation and consent journey status checks.
- Switch joint consent QR generation path to the new `consent-journey-link` API contract
- Switch joint consent status fetch path to the new `get-consent-journey-status` API contract
- Update status mapping based on new response semantics while keeping polling/retry/error behavior unchanged
- Restrict old API removal to joint-consent implementation paths only; keep legacy usage elsewhere
- Keep `mb`/`rs` wrapper construction at interceptor layer, with only `reqData` passed by feature callers
- [ ] Joint consent QR generation uses new consent journey-link contract
- [ ] Joint consent status polling uses new consent journey-status contract
- [ ] Joint consent UI states map correctly from new backend statuses
- [ ] Legacy API usage remains untouched outside joint-consent flows
- [ ] Interceptor-level wrapping remains source of truth for request envelope
- Full replacement of old consent APIs across all product journeys — excluded to avoid regressions in non-joint-consent modules
- Polling cadence, retry policy, and generic network error strategy redesign — excluded because milestone targets endpoint contract migration only
- Workspace includes multiple Flutter packages: app shell, common data, common frontend, lego blocks, and onboarding journey frontend
- Existing networking uses Dio + interceptors with envelope/auth/encryption handling centralized in common-data
- Joint consent integration depends on backend API contract changes requiring endpoint and response-mapping updates
- **Compatibility**: Keep existing behavior for non-joint-consent modules — avoid broad API migration scope
- **Architecture**: Preserve interceptor-based envelope wrapping — prevent duplicate request-shaping logic in feature layers
- **Reliability**: Maintain current polling/retry/error handling semantics — reduce behavior-change risk during contract migration
| Apply consent API contract migration only in joint-consent flows | Limits blast radius and protects other journeys still using legacy contracts | — Pending |
| Keep envelope (`mb`/`rs`) creation in interceptors | Enforces one request-shaping path and avoids drift across features | — Pending |
This document evolves at phase transitions and milestone boundaries.
1. Requirements invalidated? -> Move to Out of Scope with reason
2. Requirements validated? -> Move to Validated with phase reference
3. New requirements emerged? -> Add to Active
4. Decisions to log? -> Add to Key Decisions
5. "What This Is" still accurate? -> Update if drifted
2. Core Value check — still the right priority?
3. Audit Out of Scope — reasons still valid?
**Core value:** Critical onboarding journeys remain reliable and compliant while evolving APIs and backend contracts without user-facing disruption.
**Current focus:** Milestone v1.0 joint consent change
- Milestone initialized from existing brownfield codebase mapping artifacts under .planning/codebase/
- New scope targets joint-consent API contract migration only
- Detailed endpoint payloads were provided for QR generation and status fetch APIs

agent.description: Edit files in your workspace in agent mode
agent.name: agent

slashCommands.name: compact
slashCommands.description: Free up context by compacting the conversation history. Optionally include extra instructions for compaction.

slashCommands.name: explain
slashCommands.description: Explain how the code in your active editor works

slashCommands.name: tests
slashCommands.description: Generate unit tests for the selected code

disambiguation.description: The user wants to generate unit tests.
examples: Generate tests for my selection using pytest.

slashCommands.name: fix
slashCommands.description: Propose a fix for the problems in the selected code
slashCommands.sampleRequest: There is a problem in this code. Rewrite the code to show it with the bug fixed.

slashCommands.name: new
slashCommands.description: Scaffold code for a new file or project in a workspace
slashCommands.sampleRequest: Create a RESTful API server using typescript

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a complete Visual Studio Code workspace from scratch, such as a new application or a Visual Studio Code extension. Use this category only if the question relates to generating or creating new workspaces in Visual Studio Code. Do not use this category for updating existing code or generating sample code snippets
examples: Create a sample project which uses the fileSystemProvider API.

slashCommands.name: newNotebook
slashCommands.sampleRequest: How do I create a notebook to load data from a csv file?

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a new Jupyter notebook in Visual Studio Code.
examples: Create a notebook to analyze this CSV file.

slashCommands.name: setupTests
slashCommands.description: Set up tests in your project (Experimental)
slashCommands.sampleRequest: add playwright tests to my project

disambiguation.description: The user wants to configure project test setup, framework, or test runner. The user does not want to fix their existing tests.
examples: Set up tests for this project.

<description>**WORKFLOW SKILL** — Create, update, review, fix, or debug VS Code agent customization files (.instructions.md, .prompt.md, .agent.md, SKILL.md, copilot-instructions.md, AGENTS.md). USE FOR: saving coding preferences; troubleshooting why instructions/skills/agents are ignored or not invoked; configuring applyTo patterns; defining tool restrictions; creating custom agent modes or specialized workflows; packaging domain knowledge; fixing YAML frontmatter syntax. INVOKES: file system tools (read/write customization files), ask-questions tool (interview user for requirements), subagents for codebase exploration. <agents>
Here is a list of agents that can be used when running a subagent.
Each agent has optionally a description with the agent's purpose and expertise. When asked to run a subagent, choose the most appropriate agent from this list.
Use the #tool:agent/runSubagent tool with the agent name to run the subagent.
<agent>
<name>gsd-advisor-researcher</name>
<description>Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-assumptions-analyzer</name>
<description>Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-codebase-mapper</name>
<description>Explores codebase and writes structured analysis documents. Spawned by map-codebase with a focus area (tech, arch, quality, concerns). Writes documents directly to reduce orchestrator context load.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-debugger</name>
<description>Investigates bugs using scientific method, manages debug sessions, handles checkpoints. Spawned by /gsd:debug orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-executor</name>
<description>Executes GSD plans with atomic commits, deviation handling, checkpoint protocols, and state management. Spawned by execute-phase orchestrator or execute-plan command.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-integration-checker</name>
<description>Verifies cross-phase integration and E2E flows. Checks that phases connect properly and user workflows complete end-to-end.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-nyquist-auditor</name>
<description>Fills Nyquist validation gaps by generating tests and verifying coverage for phase requirements</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-phase-researcher</name>
<description>Researches how to implement a phase before planning. Produces RESEARCH.md consumed by gsd-planner. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-plan-checker</name>
<description>Verifies plans will achieve phase goal before execution. Goal-backward analysis of plan quality. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-planner</name>
<description>Creates executable phase plans with task breakdown, dependency analysis, and goal-backward verification. Spawned by /gsd:plan-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-project-researcher</name>
<description>Researches domain ecosystem before roadmap creation. Produces files in .planning/research/ consumed during roadmap creation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project or /gsd:new-milestone orchestrators.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-research-synthesizer</name>
<description>Synthesizes research outputs from parallel researcher agents into SUMMARY.md. Spawned by /gsd:new-project after 4 researcher agents complete.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-roadmapper</name>
<description>Creates project roadmaps with phase breakdown, requirement mapping, success criteria derivation, and coverage validation. Spawned by /gsd:new-project orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-auditor</name>
<description>Retroactive 6-pillar visual audit of implemented frontend code. Produces scored UI-REVIEW.md. Spawned by /gsd:ui-review orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-checker</name>
<description>Validates UI-SPEC.md design contracts against 6 quality dimensions. Produces BLOCK/FLAG/PASS verdicts. Spawned by /gsd:ui-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-ui-researcher</name>
<description>Produces UI-SPEC.md design contract for frontend phases. Reads upstream artifacts, detects design system state, asks only unanswered questions. Spawned by /gsd:ui-phase orchestrator.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-user-profiler</name>
<description>Analyzes extracted session messages across 8 behavioral dimensions to produce a scored developer profile with confidence levels and evidence. Spawned by profile orchestration workflows.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-verifier</name>
<description>Verifies phase goal achievement through goal-backward analysis. Checks codebase delivers what phase promised, not just that tasks completed. Creates VERIFICATION.md report.</description>
</agent>
<agent>
<name>gsd-advisor-researcher</name>
<description>Researches a single gray area decision and returns a structured comparison table with rationale. Spawned by discuss-phase advisor mode.</description>
</agent>
<agent>

<description>Deeply analyzes codebase for a phase and returns structured assumptions with evidence. Spawned by discuss-phase assumptions mode.</description>
</agent>

<agent>
<name>Explore</name>
<description>Fast read-only codebase exploration and Q&A subagent. Prefer over manually chaining multiple search and file-reading operations to avoid cluttering the main conversation. Safe to call in parallel. Specify thoroughness: quick, medium, or thorough.</description>
<argumentHint>Describe WHAT you're looking for and desired thoroughness (quick/medium/thorough)</argumentHint>
</agent>

agent.description: Edit files in your workspace in agent mode
agent.name: agent

slashCommands.name: compact
slashCommands.description: Free up context by compacting the conversation history. Optionally include extra instructions for compaction.

slashCommands.name: explain
slashCommands.description: Explain how the code in your active editor works

slashCommands.name: tests
slashCommands.description: Generate unit tests for the selected code

disambiguation.description: The user wants to generate unit tests.
examples: Generate tests for my selection using pytest.

slashCommands.name: fix
slashCommands.description: Propose a fix for the problems in the selected code
slashCommands.sampleRequest: There is a problem in this code. Rewrite the code to show it with the bug fixed.

slashCommands.name: new
slashCommands.description: Scaffold code for a new file or project in a workspace
slashCommands.sampleRequest: Create a RESTful API server using typescript

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a complete Visual Studio Code workspace from scratch, such as a new application or a Visual Studio Code extension. Use this category only if the question relates to generating or creating new workspaces in Visual Studio Code. Do not use this category for updating existing code or generating sample code snippets
examples: Create a sample project which uses the fileSystemProvider API.

slashCommands.name: newNotebook
slashCommands.sampleRequest: How do I create a notebook to load data from a csv file?

disambiguation.description: The user wants to create a new Jupyter notebook in Visual Studio Code.
examples: Create a notebook to analyze this CSV file.

slashCommands.name: setupTests
slashCommands.description: Set up tests in your project (Experimental)
slashCommands.sampleRequest: add playwright tests to my project

disambiguation.description: The user wants to configure project test setup, framework, or test runner. The user does not want to fix their existing tests.
examples: Set up tests for this project.
